'Rteft, Tinv vFqE {r{r geq qlqfr8 eTfs{,rft ffi ffi ' fit{$r} ffiT 4126 GV20t4 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA:EXTRAORDINARY IPARr II-SEC. 3(i)l hIINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFF'AIRS NOTlFICATIGN New Delhi, the 16ttr October. 2014 G.S.R.. 732{E},-In exereise of the powers conferred by ciause (a) of section 22 of the Passports Aet, 1961 (15 of 1967), the Central Government. being r:f the opinion that it is necessary anci expedient in the public interest so to do hereby exempts the passport holders whose passpbrt got darnaged or lost due to occurrence of flood in the State of Jammu and Kashilir in September, 2014, frorn payment of passport fees specrfied under section -{ of.the said Act, read with ruie 8 ofthe Passports Rules, 1980 subject to the following conditions, narnely;- (a) such exemption in pa;rment of passport fees shall be applicable only to such passport holders of Jammu and Kashmir whose passpoils are damaged or lost in the flood; (b) duplicate passports shall be issued to ali applicants desirous of availing such eiemption with the same particulars as in tlreir ptevious passports and for rhe remaining period of validitv; (c) such exemption shall remain in force for a period of six months from the date of issue of this notification. {F.
